name: Akoya Data Access Network Open Banking API
what_it_does: Akoya Data Access Network open banking REST API for US fintech developers, wealth management platforms, and financial service providers to access consumer financial data from 200+ US financial institutions through direct API connections (not screen scraping) — enabling CFPB Section 1033-compliant financial data access, token-based consent management, and standardized FDX (Financial Data Exchange) account data retrieval with bank-grade security. Enables AI agents to manage account data access for FDX-standardized financial data automation, handle consent token for user-authorized data access automation, access transaction history for spending analytics automation, retrieve investment account data for wealth management automation, manage identity data for financial account verification automation, handle bank-grade API for non-screen-scraping data access automation, access routing numbers for payment verification automation, retrieve balance data for real-time account status automation, manage token refresh for persistent consent automation, and integrate Akoya with wealth management, fintech, and personal finance platforms for CFPB-compliant financial data automation.
use_cases: Accessing consumer financial data from 200+ US banks via direct APIs for personal finance management automation agents
Implementing CFPB Section 1033-compliant financial data access with user consent management automation agents
Retrieving FDX-standardized account, transaction, and investment data for wealth management automation agents
Replacing screen scraping with bank-direct API connections for reliable financial data aggregation automation agents
not_for: Non-US financial institutions (Akoya covers US banks and brokerages only)
Payment initiation (Akoya is read-only financial data access, not payment initiation)
Real-time fraud monitoring (Akoya is account data access, not real-time transaction monitoring)
best_when: A US fintech developer or financial platform wanting AI agents to access consumer financial data from 200+ US institutions via direct bank API connections (FDX standard, not screen scraping) with CFPB Section 1033-compliant consent management.
avoid_when: FDX STANDARD REQUIRED: Akoya uses FDX (Financial Data Exchange) API standard; automated proprietary format assumption creates schema mismatch; automated must implement FDX client for Akoya data response parsing. FINTECH MUST REGISTER WITH AKOYA: Access requires approved Akoya network participant registration; automated anonymous API access assumption creates no API access; automated must complete Akoya recipient registration and certification. CONSENT TOKENS HAVE EXPIRY: Akoya data access uses OAuth-based consent tokens with expiry; automated permanent token assumption creates token_expired on reuse; automated must implement token refresh and re-consent flows. BANK COVERAGE IS EXPANDING: Akoya network is growing (200+ institutions) but does not cover all US banks; automated complete US bank coverage assumption creates institution_not_connected; automated must implement fallback aggregation for non-Akoya institutions.

auth.notes: Akoya uses OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for consent-based financial data access. REST API with FDX JSON. Charlotte, NC HQ. Founded 2018, launched 2020. Backed by Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) and major US banks. Products: FDX account data, transaction history, investment data, identity, consent token management. SDKs: None public. 200+ US financial institutions. CFPB Section 1033 aligned. FDX member. GLBA, CCPA compliant. Non-screen-scraping focus. Competes with Plaid and MX for US financial data.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox for approved network participants; production requires Akoya recipient agreement
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-API-call pricing for network participants; enterprise volume pricing
pricing.notes: Charlotte NC. FIS-backed. Network participant registration required. FDX standard. CFPB 1033 aligned.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; FDX data APIs throttled per network agreement

known_agent_gotchas: NETWORK PARTICIPANT REGISTRATION REQUIRED: Akoya requires approved recipient registration and certification before API access; automated self-service API key assumption creates no available endpoint; automated must complete Akoya recipient certification process before development

FDX SCHEMA IMPLEMENTATION REQUIRED: Akoya returns data in FDX (Financial Data Exchange) standard format; automated generic JSON parsing assumption creates schema mismatch; automated must implement FDX-compliant data models for account, transaction, and investment data

OAUTH PKCE FLOW REQUIRED: Akoya uses OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for user consent; automated server-side client credentials assumption creates authorization_code_required error; automated must implement PKCE authorization code flow for user-consented data access

INSTITUTION COVERAGE IS INCOMPLETE: Akoya covers 200+ institutions but not all US banks; automated universal US bank coverage assumption creates institution_not_connected; automated must implement fallback to traditional aggregation for banks not yet on Akoya network

CFPB 1033 DATA RIGHTS COMPLIANCE: Akoya access is governed by CFPB financial data rights; automated unrestricted data use creates regulatory risk as rules evolve; automated must monitor CFPB Section 1033 rulemaking and implement compliant data portability and deletion rights
agent_readiness.security_notes: US open banking data network. CFPB 1033, GLBA, CCPA, FDX. OAuth2 PKCE. US. Bank account and financial data. FIS-backed network.

security_score_components.security_notes: US open banking. CFPB 1033, GLBA. OAuth2 PKCE. US. Bank account and financial data. FDX standard.
